threat_model: Adapter workers can cross provider boundaries, so a signed response must not be enough to claim a live external effect. The risk is false success, omitted effect boundary evidence, tenant-scoped side-effect spoofing, replay without idempotency, missing approval or recovery evidence, secret-value disclosure, or provider observation evidence drift. The mitigation is a fail-closed receipt assessment that infers known external-write capabilities, requires retained provider observation evidence and approval for live-provider claims, requires dry-run proof fields for dry-run claims, rejects secret disclosure and forbidden-effect observations, and emits deterministic blocker codes before the gateway accepts effect-bearing worker success.

required_checks.check: authentication boundary check proving the assessment runs after response signature validation and performs no request authentication
IDOR and tenant-scope spoofing check through request, tenant, and capability receipt validation before effect assessment
external provider effect check requiring provider observation hash, provider observation ref, approval ref, and recovery evidence before live-provider success is admitted
policy bypass check rejecting plan-only external-effect claims, omitted external-write boundaries, and live-provider claims without provider evidence
receipt integrity check requiring evidence refs, idempotency key, rollback or recovery ref, sha256 provider observation hash, and no secret-value disclosure
audit visibility check through deterministic blocker codes and durable connector documentation

mitigations: infer known external-write capabilities when receipt evidence is omitted
require dry-run execution proof fields before dry-run success is admitted
require provider observation hash, provider observation ref, and approval ref before live-provider success is admitted
require idempotency key and rollback or recovery ref for effect-bearing execution claims
reject non-boolean external effect, forbidden-effect, and secret-disclosure fields
reject receipts that disclose secret values
perform no provider network call, request authentication, or credential mutation inside the assessment
